background: A manufacturer has observed that over the course of 10 months, the numbers of products demanded were $T_1, T_2, \ldots, T_{10}$, respectively. If we assume that these are i.i.d. observations of demand.

task.query: What bound can be put on the probability that their average $\bar{T}=\frac{\sum_{i=1}^{10} T_i}{10}$ is more than $3 \sigma$ products from the average demand $\mu$?
task.answer:
We obtain

$$
E(\bar{T})=\mu, \quad \operatorname{Var}(\bar{T})=\frac{\sigma^2}{10}
$$

From the Chebyshev's inequality,

$$
P\{|\bar{T}-\mu| \geq 3 \sigma\}=P\left\{\left|\frac{\bar{T}-\mu}{\sigma / \sqrt{10}}\right| \geq 3 \sqrt{10}\right\} \leq \frac{1}{(3 \sqrt{10})^2}=\frac{1}{90}
$$
task.method: 事件发生概率及独立性

task.query:
Let $X_1, X_2, \ldots, X_{\mathrm{n}}$ and $X$ be random variables on a probability space. We say that $X_{\mathrm{n}}$ converges in probability to $X$ if
$$
\begin{aligned}
&\lim _{n \rightarrow \infty} P\left(\left|X_n-X\right|<\varepsilon\right)=1 \quad \text { for every } \varepsilon>0\\
&\text { Similarly, } X_{\mathrm{n}} \text { converges with probability } 1 \text { (or almost surely) to } X \text { if }\\
&P\left(\lim _{n \rightarrow \infty} X_n=X\right)=1 .
\end{aligned}
$$
task.answer: The definitions of convergence in probability and almost sure convergence are provided in the question.
task.method: 概率空间相关（如状态数）
